description: Cardiac emergency cases for Resident Life simulator - real clinical scenarios with real lab/imaging results

patients.name: Mr. Hassan
patients.complaint: Crushing chest pain radiating to left arm for 3 hours. Diaphoretic, anxious.
patients.triageNote: 62M, chest pain, diaphoretic, HR 105, BP 155/95, SpO2 94%

result.finding: ST elevation 4mm in leads V1-V4 with reciprocal ST depression in II, III, aVF. Hyperacute T waves in V2-V4.
result.interpretation: Anteroseptal STEMI - likely proximal LAD occlusion

result.finding: hs-cTnI: 12.8 ng/mL (normal <0.04 ng/mL)
result.interpretation: Markedly elevated - confirms myocardial necrosis

result.finding: CK-MB: 85 ng/mL (normal <5 ng/mL)
result.interpretation: Elevated - consistent with MI
Mild leukocytosis - stress response

result.finding: Normal cardiac silhouette. No pulmonary edema, no pleural effusion. Mediastinum normal width.
result.interpretation: No signs of heart failure or aortic dissection

result.finding: Anterior wall hypokinesis. LVEF estimated 40%. No pericardial effusion. No aortic root dilation.
result.interpretation: Regional wall motion abnormality consistent with LAD territory ischemia

result.finding: Antiplatelet therapy initiated
result.interpretation: Essential first step in STEMI management

result.finding: Anticoagulation initiated - 60 units/kg bolus, 12 units/kg/hr infusion
result.interpretation: Standard STEMI anticoagulation

result.finding: Chest pain decreased from 8/10 to 4/10. BP 140/85
result.interpretation: Symptomatic relief achieved

possibleActions.name: Activate Cath Lab for Primary PCI

result.finding: Door-to-balloon time: 52 minutes. Successful DES placement in proximal LAD. TIMI 3 flow restored.
result.interpretation: Reperfusion successful - patient stabilized

treatmentPathway.wrongActionsConsequence: Delay in reperfusion - each minute of delay increases mortality by 1%

criticalErrors.consequence: Performing CXR before ECG and aspirin delays critical STEMI care by 12+ minutes

stages.event: Chest pain intensifying. ST elevation increasing.
Pain partially relieved but ST elevation persists.
Developing heart failure signs. Needs PCI urgently.
Cardiogenic shock. Death if no PCI.
Pain improving post-PCI. ST elevation resolving.

learningPoints.missedDiagnosis: If patient deteriorates: Consider missed aortic dissection (tearing pain, BP differential, widened mediastinum on CXR)
learningPoints.keyTeaching: Door-to-balloon time should be <90 minutes. Every 30-minute delay increases 1-year mortality by 7.5%.
learningPoints.guideline: AHA/ACC STEMI Guidelines: Immediate ECG within 10 minutes of arrival. If STEMI confirmed, activate cath lab immediately.

patients.name: Mrs. Layla
patients.complaint: Sudden severe tearing pain between shoulder blades radiating to back. BP 210/110 right arm, 165/95 left arm.
patients.triageNote: 58F, severe interscapular pain, BP differential, HR 98, looks very unwell

result.finding: Sinus tachycardia. LVH with strain pattern. No ST elevation. No ischemic changes.
result.interpretation: No evidence of acute MI. LVH consistent with chronic hypertension.

result.finding: Widened mediastinum (>8cm). Loss of aortic knob contour. Tracheal deviation to right. Left pleural effusion.
result.interpretation: Classic findings of aortic dissection/rupture

result.finding: Type A aortic dissection extending from aortic root to iliac bifurcation. Intimal flap visible. Pericardial effusion present (1.5cm). Left hemothorax.
result.interpretation: Extensive Type A dissection with tamponade physiology - SURGICAL EMERGENCY

result.finding: hs-cTnI: 0.8 ng/mL (mildly elevated)
result.interpretation: Mild elevation - may be due to coronary ostia involvement or demand ischemia

result.finding: Pericardial effusion 1.5cm with diastolic RV collapse. Aortic root dilation 5.2cm. Intimal flap in ascending aorta. Moderate AR.
result.interpretation: Cardiac tamponade physiology from Type A dissection

result.finding: Esmolol IV then nicardipine drip. BP now 120/75 bilateral. HR 65. Pain decreased.
result.interpretation: Blood pressure controlled - reduces shear stress on aortic wall

result.finding: Cardiothoracic surgeon activated. OR prepared. Patient transferred to OR within 35 minutes.
result.interpretation: Type A dissection requires emergency surgery - mortality 1-2% per hour without surgery

result.finding: WARNING: Aspirin may worsen bleeding in aortic dissection
result.interpretation: DO NOT give antiplatelets if dissection suspected - increases surgical bleeding risk

treatmentPathway.wrongActionsConsequence: Antiplatelet/anticoagulation in aortic dissection can be catastrophic

criticalErrors.consequence: Antiplatelet therapy in aortic dissection increases intraoperative bleeding and mortality
Taking dissection patient to cath lab instead of CT surgery delays definitive treatment. Mortality increases 1-2% per hour.

stages.event: Pain worsening. BP dropping. Developing shock.
BP controlled. Pain improving. OR ready.
Surgery successful. Patient stable in ICU.

learningPoints.missedDiagnosis: If you thought it was STEMI: Check BP in both arms! Pulse deficit or BP differential >20mmHg suggests dissection. CXR shows widened mediastinum.
learningPoints.keyTeaching: Type A aortic dissection mortality: 1-2% per hour without surgery. Always check bilateral BP in chest pain patients.
learningPoints.guideline: ESC Aortic Guidelines: Immediate blood pressure control (target SBP 100-120), then urgent CT angiography. Type A = emergency surgery.

patients.name: Mr. Omar
patients.complaint: Sharp chest pain, worse with deep breathing and lying flat. Better when sitting forward. Fever for 3 days.
patients.triageNote: 45M, pleuritic chest pain, positional, febrile 38.5°C, HR 105

result.finding: Diffuse ST elevation (concave upward) in leads I, II, III, aVL, aVF, V2-V6. PR depression in II, aVF, V4-V6. No reciprocal ST depression.
result.interpretation: Classic Stage I pericarditis. Diffuse ST elevation without reciprocal changes distinguishes from STEMI.

result.finding: hs-cTnI: 1.2 ng/mL (mildly elevated)
result.interpretation: Myopericarditis can cause mild troponin elevation

result.finding: CRP: 185 mg/L (markedly elevated)
result.interpretation: Significant inflammation consistent with pericarditis

result.finding: Normal cardiac silhouette. No pulmonary infiltrates. No pleural effusion. Mediastinum normal.
result.interpretation: No alternative diagnosis identified

result.finding: Normal LV function. Small pericardial effusion (0.3cm) without tamponade. No wall motion abnormalities.
result.interpretation: Pericardial effusion supports pericarditis diagnosis

result.finding: Pain significantly improved within 1 hour. Patient comfortable sitting upright.
result.interpretation: Response to NSAIDs is characteristic of pericarditis

result.finding: Added to NSAIDs for recurrence prevention
result.interpretation: Colchicine reduces recurrence rate by 50% (CORI trial)

treatmentPathway.wrongActionsConsequence: Unnecessary STEMI protocol activation causes patient anxiety and resource waste

stages.event: Stable for discharge on NSAIDs + colchicine.

learningPoints.missedDiagnosis: If treated as STEMI: Diffuse ST elevation without reciprocal changes is pericarditis, not STEMI. Cath lab activation unnecessary.
learningPoints.keyTeaching: Pericarditis ECG: Diffuse concave ST elevation + PR depression. STEMI: Localized convex ST elevation + reciprocal changes.
learningPoints.guideline: ESC Pericarditis Guidelines: First-line = NSAIDs + colchicine for 3 months. Steroids only if refractory.

patients.name: Mrs. Samira
patients.complaint: Sudden onset severe dyspnea, pleuritic chest pain, near-syncope. Recent hip surgery 10 days ago.
patients.triageNote: 72F, acute dyspnea, pleuritic pain, HR 125, BP 88/55, SpO2 85% on RA, recent surgery
patients.correctDiagnosis: Massive Pulmonary Embolism with Obstructive Shock

result.finding: Sinus tachycardia. S1Q3T3 pattern (deep S in I, Q wave and inverted T in III). Right axis deviation. Incomplete RBBB. T wave inversion V1-V4.
result.interpretation: Classic right heart strain pattern of massive PE

result.finding: pH 7.32, pCO2 32, pO2 52, HCO3 18, Lactate 4.8, SaO2 84%
result.interpretation: Type 1 respiratory failure with metabolic acidosis (lactic). Severe hypoxemia.

result.finding: hs-cTnI: 2.4 ng/mL
result.interpretation: RV strain causing myocardial injury

result.finding: D-Dimer: >10,000 ng/mL (massively elevated)
result.interpretation: Extremely high - but NOT needed in high pre-test probability with shock

result.finding: Large bilateral central pulmonary emboli. Saddle embolus at bifurcation. RV:LV ratio 1.4. Interventricular septal bowing.
result.interpretation: Massive PE with right heart strain - HIGH RISK

result.finding: Severely dilated RV. McConnell's sign (apical sparing). D-shaped LV in systole. PAP estimated 60mmHg. No pericardial effusion.
result.interpretation: Acute right heart strain consistent with massive PE

result.finding: tPA administered. BP improved to 110/70. SpO2 92%. HR 100. Hemodynamics stabilizing.
result.interpretation: Thrombolysis indicated in massive PE with shock. Clinical improvement noted.

result.finding: Anticoagulation initiated after thrombolysis
result.interpretation: Heparin continued after tPA to prevent re-thrombosis

result.finding: 500mL crystalloid given slowly. BP transiently improved but careful monitoring needed.
result.interpretation: Fluids can worsen RV failure in massive PE - use cautiously

treatmentPathway.wrongActionsConsequence: Delay in thrombolysis in massive PE with shock increases mortality significantly

criticalErrors.consequence: D-dimer testing in high-risk patients with shock delays life-saving treatment. Proceed directly to imaging or bedside echo.

stages.event: tPA working. BP improving. Oxygenation better.

learningPoints.missedDiagnosis: If missed: PE is the great masquerader. Always ask about risk factors (recent surgery, immobility, cancer, OCP). Bedside echo showing RV strain = treat as PE until proven otherwise.
learningPoints.keyTeaching: Massive PE = PE with sustained hypotension (SBP <90 for >15 min) or requiring inotropes. Thrombolysis is first-line, NOT just anticoagulation.
learningPoints.guideline: ESC PE Guidelines: High-risk PE (shock/hypotension) → immediate thrombolysis unless contraindicated. Do NOT delay for CT if bedside echo available.

patients.name: Mr. Ibrahim
patients.complaint: Progressive dyspnea over 3 days, orthopnea, PND, bilateral leg swelling. History of CAD, previous MI, diabetes.
patients.triageNote: 68M, respiratory distress, orthopnea, bilateral edema, HR 115, BP 145/90, SpO2 88% on RA, crackles bilaterally
patients.correctDiagnosis: Acute Decompensated Heart Failure with Reduced Ejection Fraction (ADHFrEF)

result.finding: Sinus tachycardia. Old anteroseptal Q waves. LVH. No acute ST changes.
result.interpretation: No acute ischemia. Evidence of prior MI and LVH consistent with chronic HF.

result.finding: Cardiomegaly. Bilateral interstitial and alveolar opacities. Kerley B lines. Small bilateral pleural effusions. Cephalization of pulmonary vessels.
result.interpretation: Classic pulmonary edema from congestive heart failure

result.finding: pH 7.34, pCO2 48, pO2 58, HCO3 26, Lactate 2.1, SaO2 87%
result.interpretation: Type 2 respiratory failure (hypercapnic) with mild metabolic acidosis. Hypoxemia from pulmonary edema.

result.finding: NT-proBNP: 8,500 pg/mL (markedly elevated)
result.interpretation: Very high BNP confirms heart failure as cause of dyspnea

result.finding: hs-cTnI: 0.15 ng/mL (mildly elevated, stable)
result.interpretation: Chronic mild elevation from demand ischemia in HF. No acute MI pattern.

result.finding: LVEF 25%. Severe global hypokinesis. Dilated LV. Moderate MR. No pericardial effusion. IVC dilated >2.1cm with <50% collapse.
result.interpretation: Severe systolic dysfunction. IVC findings indicate significantly elevated CVP.

result.finding: Urine output 1.2L in 2 hours. Respiratory status improving. Crackles decreasing. SpO2 93% on 2L O2.
result.interpretation: Good diuretic response. Pulmonary edema resolving.

result.finding: BP reduced to 130/80. Preload and afterload reduction achieved. Dyspnea improved.
result.interpretation: Vasodilator therapy effective in acute pulmonary edema

result.finding: BiPAP IPAP 14/EPAP 6, FiO2 0.6. SpO2 improved to 96%. Work of breathing decreased. pCO2 improving.
result.interpretation: NIV effective for acute cardiogenic pulmonary edema with hypercapnia

treatmentPathway.wrongActionsConsequence: IV fluid bolus in pulmonary edema will worsen respiratory failure

criticalErrors.consequence: Fluid bolus in acute pulmonary edema can precipitate intubation and ICU admission

stages.event: Increasing respiratory distress. SpO2 dropping below 85%.
Severe hypercapnia. Altered mental status. Impending respiratory arrest.
Crackles resolving. Patient comfortable on 2L NC.
Stable for admission to cardiology ward.

learningPoints.missedDiagnosis: If you thought it was COPD: Bilateral crackles + orthopnea + leg edema = heart failure. CXR with pulmonary edema confirms. COPD doesn't cause crackles.
learningPoints.keyTeaching: Acute cardiogenic pulmonary edema treatment: Lasix + Nitrates + NIV if hypercapnic. DO NOT give IV fluids. Check ejection fraction to guide long-term therapy.
learningPoints.guideline: ESC Heart Failure Guidelines: IV loop diuretics are first-line for acute pulmonary edema. Add vasodilators if hypertensive. NIV reduces intubation rate by 50%.

conceptsTested: ECG interpretation (STEMI vs Pericarditis vs PE vs Normal)
Chest X-Ray interpretation (Pulmonary edema, Widened mediastinum, Normal)
